author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though chiefly the Lord’s Passion is noticed in this Psalm, neither could the Martyrs have been strong, unless they had beheld Him, that first suffered; nor such things would they have endured in suffering, as He did, unless they had hoped for such things in the Resurrection as He had showed of Himself: but your Holiness knoweth that our Head is our Lord Jesus Christ, and that all that cleave unto Him are the members of Him the Head .…And let no one say, that now-a-days in tribulation of passions we are not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For alway ye have heard this fact, how in those times the whole Church together as it were was smitten against, but now through individuals she is tried.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bound indeed is the devil, that he may not do as much as he could, that he may not do as much as he would: nevertheless, he is permitted to tempt as much as is expedient to men advancing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not expedient for us to be without temptations: nor should we beseech God that we be not tempted, but that we be not “led into temptation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say we, therefore, ourselves also: “Hearken, O God, to my prayer, while I am troubled; from fear of the enemy deliver my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Enemies have raged against the Martyrs: for what was that voice of Christ’s Body praying?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this it was praying, to be delivered from enemies, and that enemies might not have power to slay them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were they not therefore hearkened to, because they were slain; and hath God forsaken His servants of a contrite heart, and despised men hoping in Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “who hath called upon God, and hath been forsaken; who hath hoped in Him, and hath been deserted by Him?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were hearkened to therefore, and they were slain; and yet from enemies they were delivered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Others being afraid gave consent, and lived, and yet the same by enemies were swallowed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The slain were delivered, the living were swallowed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is also that voice of thanksgiving, “Perchance alive they would have swallowed us up.”…Therefore for this prayeth the voice of the Martyrs, “From fear of the enemy deliver Thou my soul:” not so that the enemy may not slay me, but that I may not fear an enemy slaying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that to be fulfilled in the Psalm the servant prayeth, which but now in the Gospel the Lord was commanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What but now was the Lord commanding?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fear not them that kill the body, but the soul are not able to kill; but Him rather fear ye, that hath power to kill both body and soul in the hell of fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He repeated, “Yea, I say unto you, fear Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that kill the body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was the Lord commanding?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they should not be feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be prayer offered, therefore, that He may grant what He hath commanded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From fear of the enemy deliver my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deliver me from fear of the enemy, and make me submit to the fear of Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would not fear him that killeth the body, but I would fear Him that hath power to kill both body and soul in the hell of fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not from fear would I be free: but from fear of the enemy being free, under fear of the Lord a servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast protected me from the gathering together of malignants, and from the multitude of men working iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now upon Himself our Head let us look.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Like things many Martyrs have suffered: but nothing doth shine out so brightly as the Head of Martyrs; in Him rather let us behold what they have gone through.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Protected He was from the multitude of malignants, God protecting Himself, the Son Himself and the Manhood which He was carrying protecting His flesh: because Son of Man He is, and Son of God He is; Son of God because of the form of God, Son of Man because of the form of a servant: having in His power to lay down His life: and to take it again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To Him what could enemies do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They killed body, soul they killed not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Too little therefore it were for the Lord to exhort the Martyrs with word, unless He had enforced it by example.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know what a gathering together there was of malignant Jews, and what a multitude there was of men working iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That wherewith they willed to kill the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “So many good works,” He saith, “I have shown to you, for which of these will ye to kill Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He endured all their infirm, He healed all their sick, He preached the Kingdom of Heaven, He held not His peace at their vices, so that these same should have been displeasing to them, rather than the Physician by whom they were being made whole: for all these His remedies being ungrateful, like men delirious in high fever raving at the physician, they devised the plan of destroying Him that had come to heal them; as though therein they would prove whether He were indeed a man, that could die, or were somewhat above men, and would not suffer Himself to die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The word of these same men we perceive in the wisdom of Solomon: “with death most vile,” say they, “let us condemn Him; let us question Him, for there will be regard in the discourses of Him; for if truly Son of God He is, let Him deliver Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see therefore what was done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they have whet like a sword their tongues” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which saith another Psalm also, “Sons of men; their teeth are arms and arrows, and their tongue is a sharp sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the Jews say, we have not killed Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this end they gave Him to Pilate the judge, in order that they themselves might seem as it were guiltless of His death.…But if he is guilty because he did it though unwillingly, are they innocent who compelled him to do it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he gave sentence against Him, and commanded Him to be crucified: and in a manner himself killed Him; ye also, O ye Jews, killed Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence did ye kill Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With the sword of the tongue: for ye did whet your tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when did ye smite, except when ye cried out, “Crucify, Crucify”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But on this account we must not pass over that which hath come into mind, lest perchance the reading of the Divine Scriptures should disquiet any one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One Evangelist saith that the Lord was crucified at the sixth hour, and another at the third hour: unless we understand it, we are disquieted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when the sixth hour was already beginning, Pilate is said to have sat on the judgment-seat: and in reality when the Lord was lifted up upon the tree, it was the sixth hour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But another Evangelist, looking unto the mind of the Jews, how they wished themselves to seem guiltless of the death of the Lord, by his account proveth them guilty, saying, that the Lord was crucified at the third hour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But considering all the circumstance of the history, how many things might have been done, when before Pilate the Lord was being accused, in order that He might be crucified; we find that it might have been the third hour, when they cried out, “Crucify, Crucify.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore with more truth they killed at the time when they cried out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The ministers of the magistrate at the sixth hour crucified, the transgressors of the law at the third hour cried out: that which those did with hands at the sixth hour, these did with tongue at the third hour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More guilty are they that with crying out were raging, than they that in obedience were ministering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the whole of the Jews’ sagacity, this is that which they sought as some great matter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us kill and let us not kill: so let us kill, as that we may not ourselves be judged to have killed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have bended the bow, a bitter thing, in order that they may shoot in secret One unspotted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bow he calleth lyings in wait.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that with sword fighteth hand to hand, openly fighteth: he that shooteth an arrow deceiveth, in order to strike.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the arrow smiteth, before it is foreseen to come to wound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whom could the lyings in wait of the human heart escape?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would they escape our Lord Jesus Christ, who had no need that any one should bear witness to Him of man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Himself knew what was in man,” as the Evangelist testifieth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, let us hear them, and look upon them in their doings as if the Lord knew not what they devise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The expression he used, “They have bended the bow,” is the same as, “in secret:” as if they were deceiving by lyings in wait.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye know by what artifices they did this, how with money they bribed a disciple that clave to Him, in order that He might be betrayed to them, how they procured false witnesses; with what lyings in wait and artifices they wrought, “in order that they might shoot in secret One unspotted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great iniquity!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold from a secret place there cometh an arrow, which striketh One unspotted, who had not even so much of spot as could be pierced with an arrow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Lamb indeed He is unspotted, wholly unspotted, alway unspotted; not one from whom spots have been removed but that hath contracted not any spots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath made many unspotted by forgiving sins, being Himself unspotted by not having sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Suddenly they shall shoot Him, and shall not fear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O heart hardened, to wish to kill a Man that did raise the dead!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Suddenly:” that is, insidiously, as if unexpectedly, as if not foreseen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord was like to one knowing not, being among men knowing not what He knew not and what He knew: yea, knowing not that there was nothing that He knew not, and that He knew all things, and to this end had come in order that they might do that which they thought they did by their own power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have confirmed to themselves malignant discourse” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There were done so great miracles, they were not moved, they persisted in the design of the evil discourse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was given up to the judge: the judge trembleth, and they tremble not that have given Him up to the judge: trembleth power, and ferocity trembleth not: he would wash his hands, and they stain their tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have confirmed to themselves malignant discourse.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many things did Pilate, how many things that they might be restrained!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What said he? what did he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “they have confirmed to themselves malignant discourse: Crucify, crucify.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The repetition is the confirmation of the “malignant discourse.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see in what manner “they have confirmed to themselves malignant discourse.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Your King shall I crucify?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They said, “We have no king but Cæsar alone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was offering for King the Son of God: to a man they betook themselves: worthy were they to have the one, and not have the Other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I find not anything in this Man,” saith the judge, “wherefore He is worthy of death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they that “confirmed malignant discourse,” said, “His blood be upon us and upon our sons.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They confirmed malignant discourse,” not to the Lord, but to “themselves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how not to themselves when they say, “Upon us and upon our sons”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which therefore they confirmed, to themselves they confirmed: because the same voice is elsewhere, “They dug before my face a ditch, and fell into it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Death killed not the Lord, but He death: but them iniquity killed, because they would not kill iniquity.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They told, in order that they might hide traps: they said, Who shall see them?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They thought they would escape Him, whom they were killing, that they would escape God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, suppose Christ was a man, like the rest of men, and knew not what was being contrived for Him: doth God also know not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O heart of man! wherefore hast thou said to thyself, Who seeth me? when He seeth that hath made thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They said, Who shall see them?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God did see, Christ also was seeing: because Christ is also God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore did they think that He saw not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the words following.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have searched out iniquity, they have failed, searching searchings” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6): that is, deadly and acute designs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him not be betrayed by us, but by His disciple: let Him not be killed by us, but by the judge: let us do all, and let us seem to have done nothing.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what befell them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They failed searching searchings.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he saith, “Who shall see them?” that is, that no one saw them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This they were saying, this among themselves they thought, that no one saw them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what befalleth an evil soul: it departeth from the light of truth, and because itself seeth not God, it thinketh that itself is not seen by God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall draw near a man and a deep heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They said, Who shall see us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They failed in searching searchings, evil counsels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There drew near a man to those same counsels, He suffered Himself to be held as a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He would not have been held except He were man, or have been seen except He were man, or have been smitten except He were man, or have been crucified or have died except He were man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There drew near a man therefore to all those sufferings, which in Him would have been of no avail except He were Man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if He were not Man, there would not have been deliverance for man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There hath drawn near a Man “and a deep heart,” that is, a secret “heart:” presenting before human faces Man, keeping within God: concealing the “form of God,” wherein He is equal with the Father, and presenting the form of a servant, wherein He is less than the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Himself hath spoken of both: but one thing there is which He saith in the form of God, another thing in the form of a servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath said in the form of God, “I and the Father are one:” He hath said in the form of a servant, “For the Father is greater than I.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence in the form of God saith He, “I and the Father are one”?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arrows of infants have been made the strokes of them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is that savageness? where is that roar of the lion, of the people roaring and saying, “Crucify, Crucify”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where are the lyings in wait of men bending the bow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have not “the strokes of them been made the arrows of infants”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know in what manner infants make to themselves arrows of little canes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do they strike, or whence do they strike?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the hand, or what the weapon? what are the arms, or what the limbs?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the tongues of them have been made weak upon them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them whet now their tongues like a sword, let them confirm to themselves malignant discourse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly to themselves they have confirmed it, because “the tongues of them have been made weak upon them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Could this be strong against God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Iniquity,” he saith, “hath lied to itself;” “their tongues have been made weak upon them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, the Lord hath risen, that was killed.…What thinkest thou of Him who from the cross came not down, and from the tomb rose again?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore did they effect?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even if the Lord had not risen again, what would they have effected, except what the persecutors of the martyrs have also effected?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Martyrs have not yet risen again, and nevertheless they have effected nothing; of them not yet rising again we are now celebrating the nativities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is the madness of their raging?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what did they bring those their searchings, in which searchings they failed, so that even, when the Lord was dead and buried, they set guards at the tomb?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they said to Pilate, “That deceiver;” by this name the Lord Jesus Christ was called, for the comfort of His servants when they are called deceivers; they say therefore to Pilate, “That deceiver said when yet living, After three days I will rise again:” …They set for guards soldiers at the sepulchre.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At the earth quaking, the Lord rose again: such miracles were done about the sepulchre, that even the very soldiers that had come for guards were made witnesses, if they chose to tell the truth: but the same covetousness which had led captive a disciple, the companion of Christ, led captive also the soldier that was guard of the sepulchre.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We give you, they say, money; and say ye, while yourselves were sleeping there came His disciples, and took Him away.…Sleeping witnesses ye adduce: truly thou thyself hast fallen asleep, that in searching such devices hast failed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they were sleeping, what could they see? if nothing they saw, how are they witnesses?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “they failed in searching searchings:” failed of the light of God, failed in the very completion of their designs: when that which they willed, nowise they were able to complete, surely they failed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “there drew near a Man and a deep heart, and God was exalted.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And every man feared” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that feared not, were not even men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Every man feared;” that is, every one using reason to perceive the things which were done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence they that feared not, must rather be called cattle, rather beasts savage and cruel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A lion ramping and roaring is that people as yet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in truth every man feared: that is, they that would believe, that trembled at the judgment to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And every man feared: and they declared the works of God.”…“And every man hath feared: and they have declared the works of God, and His doings they have perceived.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “His doings they have perceived”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it, O Lord Jesus Christ, that Thou wast silent, and like a sheep for a victim wast being led, and didst not open before the shearer Thy mouth, and we thought Thee to be set in smiting and in grief, and knowing how to bear weakness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it that Thou wast hiding Thy beauty, O Thou beautiful in form before the sons of men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it that Thou didst not seem to have beauty nor grace?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst bear on the Cross men reviling and saying, “If Son of God He is, let Him come down from the Cross.”…This thing they, that would have had Him come down from the Cross, perceived not: but when He rose again, and being glorified ascended into Heaven, they perceived the works of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The just man shall rejoice in the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the just man is not sad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sad were the disciples at the Lord’s being crucified; overcome with sadness, sorrowing they departed, they thought they had lost hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He rose again, even when appearing to them He found them sad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He held the eyes of two men that walked in the way, so that by them he was not known, and He found them groaning and sighing, and He held them until He had expounded the Scriptures, and by the same Scriptures had shown that so it ought to have been done as it was done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He showed in the Scriptures, how after the third day it behoved the Lord to rise again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how on the third day would He have risen again, if from the Cross He had come down?…Therefore let us all rejoice in the Lord, let us all after the faith be One Just Man, and let us all in one Body hold One Head, and let us rejoice in the Lord, not in ourselves: because our Good is not ourselves to ourselves, but He that hath made us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself is our good to make us glad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And let no one rejoice in himself, no one rely on himself, no one despair of himself: let no one rely on any man, whom he ought to bring in to be the partner of his own hope, not the giver of the hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now because the Lord hath risen again, now because He hath ascended into Heaven, now because He hath showed that there is another life, now because it is evident that His counsels, wherein He lay concealed in deep heart, were not empty, because to this end That Blood was shed to be the price of the redeemed; now because all things are evident, because all things have been preached, because all things have been believed, under the whole of heaven, “the just man shall rejoice in the Lord, and shall hope in Him; and all men shall be praised that are right in heart.”…God is displeasing to thee, and thou art pleasing to thyself, of perverted and crooked heart thou art: and this is the worse, that the heart of God thou wouldest correct by thy heart, to make Him do what thou wilt have whereas thou oughtest to do what He willeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wouldest make crooked the heart of God which alway is right, according to the depravity of thy own heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much better to correct thy heart by the rectitude of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath not thy Lord taught thee this, of Whose Passion but now were we speaking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was He not bearing thy weakness, when He said, “Sad is My soul even unto death”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was He not figuring thyself in Himself, when He was saying, “Father, if it be possible, let there pass from Me this cup”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the hearts of the Father and of the Son were not two and different: but in the form of a servant He carried thy heart, that He might teach it by His example.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now behold trouble found out as it were another heart of thine, which willed that there should pass away that which was impending: but God would not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God consenteth not to thy heart, do thou consent to the heart of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If “there shall be praised all men right in heart,” there shall be condemned the crooked in heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Two things are set before thee now, choose while there is time.…If of crooked heart thou hast become, there will come that Judgment, there will appear all the reasons on account of which God doeth all these things: and thou that wouldest not in this life correct thy heart by the rectitude of God, and prepare thyself for the right hand, where “there shall be praised all men right in heart,” wilt be on the left, where at that time thou shalt hear, “Go ye into fire everlasting, that hath been prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And will there be then time to correct the heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore correct, brethren, now correct.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who doth hinder?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Psalm is chanted, Gospel is read, Reader crieth, Preacher crieth; long-suffering is the Lord; thou sinnest, and He spareth; still thou sinnest, still He spareth, and still thou addest sin to sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How long is God long-suffering?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt find God just also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We terrify because we fear; teach us not to fear, and we terrify no more.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But better it is that God teach us to fear, than that any man teach us not to fear.…Thou bringest forth grain, barn expect thou; bringest forth thorns, fire expect thou.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But not yet hath come either the time of the barn or the time of the fire: now let there be preparation, and there will not be fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the name of Christ both we who speak are living, and ye to whom we speak are living: for amending our plan, and changing evil life into a good life, is there no place, is there no time?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can it not, if thou wilt, be done to-day?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can it not, if thou wilt, be now done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What must thou buy in order to do it, what specifics must thou seek?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what Indies must thou sail?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What ship prepare?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, while I am speaking, change the heart; and there is done what so often and so long while is cried out for, that it be done, and which bringeth forth everlasting punishment if it be not done.
